meta.title: AI Market State Map taxonomy
meta.framework: Jensen Huang 5-layer AI cake (Energy -> Chips -> Infrastructure -> Models -> Applications)
meta.softwareThreePointZero: End-state: one research/rating Category Agent per leaf category, rolling up into Layer Agents and a Market-State Orchestrator.
meta.maintenanceModel: This file is the DURABLE CONTRACT (structure), not a data store. Human-governed + versioned: the 5 layers, category ids/names/membership, the 6 rating dimensions, and the scorecard schema. Swarm-maintained, NOT authoritative here: each category's constituents and live metric values — 'seedConstituents' and the metric lists below are only starting examples the Category Agents discover, extend, and keep fresh. Only STRUCTURAL changes (add / retire / merge a category) go through the Part 16 human gate; day-to-day constituent and metric churn never touches this file.
meta.metricsAuthority: registry/indicators.json is the authoritative source for indicator/metric scoring metadata; this file is structure only.

dimensions.label: Momentum
dimensions.desc: Growth rate of the category's core quantity (revenue, GW, capacity, share).

dimensions.label: Competitive structure
dimensions.desc: Concentration (HHI / share of #1), number of credible players.

dimensions.label: Moat & defensibility
dimensions.desc: Switching cost, ecosystem lock-in, data/network effects.

dimensions.label: Bottleneck / supply constraint
dimensions.desc: Is this the gating layer right now? lead times, queues.

scoringRubric.scale: five-word rating per dimension: Very strong | Strong | Mixed | Weak | Very weak, each with a direction (improving | steady | worsening) and a confidence (low | medium | high) — never an invented 0-100 score (Part 17)
scoringRubric.rollup: category ratings -> layer rating (by weakest link) -> one plain market status word (see layerFlags); no composite number on the cover, just the status + bottleneck + direction + reason (Part 17)

scorecardSchema.metrics: object keyed by metric id -> { value, unit, trend, source } (measured facts, reported as-is)
scorecardSchema.dimensionRatings: object keyed by rubric dimension id -> { rating: Very strong | Strong | Mixed | Weak | Very weak, direction: improving | steady | worsening, confidence: low | medium | high, findingIds: string[], rationale: string }

modularity.note: Part 18: agents are one template + an assignment. The schemas below are the durable contract; the registries are seeded thin and swarm-maintained (Part 16). Registered items are referenced by id; a provisional item may be defined inline by an agent but never feeds canonical state until promoted.

assignmentSchema.mode: canonical (taxonomy-driven, MECE, feeds official status) | adhoc (custom scope, views/answers only)
assignmentSchema.scope: { entities: entityId[], metrics: metricId[], topicLens: string|null, sources: string[]|null }
assignmentSchema.explore: { enabled: bool, budgetShare: 0..1 } (room to look beyond scope and surface provisional items)

entitySchema.name: string
entitySchema.primaryCategory: categoryId (owns it for rollup so it is counted once)

modularity.promotion: provisional -> persists & corroborated (Part 10 signal test) -> proposed -> reviewed -> registered (prune is the reverse). Initial build = LOOSE: new category needs human approval before landing (Part 16); new metric/entity auto-proposes into a review queue cleared after the fact.

categories.name: Eval / benchmark / model-hub infra
categories.note: Primary data sources for this platform's own agents.
